reasoning: There is currently no approved vaccine for Filariasis (lymphatic or otherwise). A search for recent developments shows that as of March 2024, researchers at the University of Illinois College of Medicine received a grant merely to move a lymphatic filariasis vaccine candidate "closer to human clinical trials", noting it is work towards the "first vaccine" for the disease. Given that the timeline from preclinical or early Phase 1 trials to a fully approved and recognized vaccine takes several years at an absolute minimum, it is functionally impossible for a vaccine to be fully developed, approved, and recorded on the Wikipedia 'List_of_infectious_diseases' page before the final resolution date in November 2025. Therefore, the probability is at the minimum allowable floor for all resolution dates.
